star.story.the_fool:
Atop the mist-shrouded train, there stands a figure holding a tattered deck of tarot cards, calling themselves "The Fool."

No one knows their true name. No one has seen the face behind the mask. They always stand in the deepest corner of the carriage, eyes peering through narrow slits, watching every passing traveler.

"Do you believe in fate?" they ask.

Most laugh and shake their heads. But some stop, drawn by those unfathomable eyes.

"Fate can be changed," The Fool whispers, "but first you must see its shape."

Then they offer a note — golden, covered in symbols no one can read. They call it the "Honored Note," a key to what lies above the mist.

Those who accept the note and pray become members of the "Tarot Assembly." When summoned, their souls leave their bodies and gather in a realm beyond reality. There, they vote to identify the one marked by fate — the "Heretic."

And The Fool's ancient pistol fires only at the Heretic.

"I am no judge," says The Fool. "I am merely fate's executor."

"The journey is not yet over."
